series.title: hw/riscv/virt-acpi-build.c: Use kvm timer frequency when kvm enabled

review.summary: The patch is narrowly scoped and the functional change is consistent with existing RISC-V/KVM behavior described in the commit message: RHCT should report the actual KVM timebase rather than the fixed ACLINT default. I did not identify a correctness issue in the patch itself. The only reported checkpatch issue is a line-length warning, which is non-blocking here.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear: RHCT currently hardcodes RISCV_ACLINT_DEFAULT_TIMEBASE_FREQ, which is incorrect for KVM guests where the timer frequency is host-provided.
stages.B: Code change is minimal and logically correct: when KVM is enabled, RHCT uses kvm_riscv_get_timebase_frequency(&s->soc->harts[0]); otherwise it preserves the existing default.
stages.C: Build risk appears low from the patch alone. The added includes and helper usage are architecture-specific but aligned with the stated goal. No new behavioral surface outside RHCT generation is introduced.
stages.D: Runtime behavior should improve guest-visible ACPI correctness for KVM-backed RISC-V virt machines. The change also aligns ACPI behavior with the existing device-tree handling referenced in the commit message.
stages.E: Recommendation: accept. No blocking functional issues found in this patch. Residual risk is limited to integration details outside the provided diff, which are not indicated as problematic here.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
